content:
Tutoring Prices in South Africa 2026

How much does tutoring cost in South Africa? Whether you're looking for a private tutor for yourself or your child, understanding current tutoring prices helps you budget and find the best value. This guide covers tutoring costs for all options — in-person tutors, online tutors, and AI tutoring — in 2026.

Tutoring Prices Overview: South Africa 2026

Tutoring TypeCostBest For
In-person private tutor (student)R150–R250/hourGrade 8–10, basic subjects
In-person private tutor (qualified teacher)R300–R500/hourGrade 11–12, matric prep
Online human tutorR200–R450/hourAny grade, flexible scheduling
Tutoring centre (e.g., Kumon, Educate)R500–R1,500/monthRegular structured tutoring
Group tutoring (3–5 students)R100–R200/student/hourCost-sharing with friends
AI tutoring (StudyBuddy)FREE / R99/month unlimitedAll grades, all subjects, 24/7

Tutoring Prices by Grade Level

Grade LevelTypical Hourly RateWhy
Grade 8–9 (Foundation)R150–R300/hourLess specialist knowledge required
Grade 10R200–R350/hourCAPS curriculum foundations
Grade 11R250–R450/hourIncreased complexity
Grade 12 / MatricR300–R600/hourNSC exam prep, highest demand
University (undergraduate)R350–R700/hourSpecialist knowledge, higher level

Tutoring Prices by Subject

SubjectTypical Rate (per hour)
Mathematics (Grade 10–12)R300–R600
Physical SciencesR300–R550
AccountingR280–R500
EnglishR200–R400
Life SciencesR250–R450
EconomicsR250–R450
Business StudiesR200–R400
Geography/HistoryR180–R350

Monthly Tutoring Budget Estimates

At 2 sessions per week (1.5 hours each):

Tutor RateMonthly Cost (8 sessions × 1.5h)Annual Cost (40 weeks)
R200/hourR2,400/monthR12,000/year
R300/hourR3,600/monthR18,000/year
R400/hourR4,800/monthR24,000/year
R500/hourR6,000/monthR30,000/year
StudyBuddy AIR99/month (unlimited)R1,188/year

What Affects Tutoring Prices?

  • Location: Johannesburg (Sandton, Rosebank) and Cape Town (Southern Suburbs, Claremont) charge highest. Smaller towns and rural areas charge less
  • Subject difficulty: Maths and Sciences command premium rates
  • Grade level: Matric tutors charge more than junior secondary tutors
  • Tutor qualifications: SACE-registered teachers with 10+ years' experience charge more
  • Session format: Online sessions are typically 10–20% cheaper than in-person sessions
  • Session frequency: Some tutors offer package discounts for 3+ sessions/week

How to Find a Tutor in South Africa

  • Tutoring platforms: Superprof, Tuta-Me, ClassPass, LearnPick
  • School recommendations: Ask your teacher or school for recommended tutors
  • Community groups: WhatsApp/Facebook neighbourhood groups often have tutor recommendations
  • University notice boards: University students often advertise tutoring services at very competitive rates

AI Tutoring: Best Value in 2026

For families who want quality tutoring at an affordable price, AI tutoring offers exceptional value:

  • StudyBuddy — FREE to start (no credit card required)
  • R99/month for unlimited tutoring in ALL CAPS subjects (Grade 8–12)
  • Available 24/7 — get help at any time, even midnight before an exam
  • All subjects covered: Maths, Sciences, English, Accounting, Economics, and more
  • Personalized explanations — asks what you don't understand and explains it differently

Most students using StudyBuddy improve their marks by 15–25% within a few weeks — at a fraction of the cost of human tutoring.
quickAnswer: Tutoring prices in South Africa range from R150–R600 per hour for private in-person tutors, depending on grade, subject, and location. For an affordable alternative, AI tutoring (like StudyBuddy) starts free and costs just R99/month for unlimited help in all subjects.

faqs.question: How much does a private tutor cost in South Africa in 2026?
faqs.answer: Private tutors in South Africa typically charge R150–R600 per hour depending on their qualifications, the subject, grade level, and your location. Grade 12 Maths/Science tutors in Johannesburg typically charge R350–R600/hour, while university student tutors in smaller towns may charge R150–R250/hour.

faqs.question: What is the cheapest tutoring option in South Africa?
faqs.answer: The most affordable tutoring option is AI tutoring. StudyBuddy offers free access with all subjects for Grade 8–12 students. The paid plan is just R99/month for unlimited tutoring — compared to R3,000–R6,000/month for traditional tutoring twice a week.

faqs.question: How much does online tutoring cost vs in-person tutoring?
faqs.answer: Online human tutors typically charge R200–R450/hour, which is about 10–20% cheaper than in-person rates. AI tutoring (like StudyBuddy) is significantly cheaper at R99/month for unlimited access to all subjects — no travel required.

faqs.question: Is tutoring worth the cost?
faqs.answer: Yes — especially for Grade 11 and 12 students preparing for matric exams. Students who receive regular tutoring typically improve their marks by 10–30%. However, you don't need to spend R3,000–R6,000/month on tutoring. Many students get excellent results with AI tutoring at just R99/month.
